title: Bridge Essay: The salience network is activated during self-recognition from both first-person

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through The, salience, network, is, activated.

**The salience network is activated during self-recognition from both first-person and third-person perspectives.** — Hum Brain Mapp. PMID: 36129447. DOI: 10.1002/hbm.26084. We usually observe ourselves from two perspectives. One is the first-person perspective, which we perceive directly with our own eyes, and the other is the third-person perspective, which we observe ourselves in amirror or apicture. However, whether the self-recognition associated with these two perspectives has acommon or separate neural basis remains unclear.
